All 5 tests in file pass against the canonical authority.
The legacy root-level schemas/ directory is purged (absent or empty of JSON schemas).
claims.note: Directory still contains 55 legacy schema JSON files. Blocks closure. Remediation remaining: Action 3 (delete 4 overlapping duplicates — pending explicit user confirmation due to content-drift audit), Action 5 (migrate orphan schemas: K8, HAL, kernel, builders, oracle_town), Action 6 (audit migrated schemas for dead refs), Action 7 (migrate Registry B consumers to governance imports), Action 8 (delete Registry B), Action 9 (delete root schemas/).

note: V5 retracts V4 and extends the receipt chain with Actions 1 and 4 (completed non-destructively per HAL decision SCHEMA-AUTHORITY-2026-04-16 cum_hash 70c69307bce0). Registry B now defaults to the canonical tree (C5). Two remaining bare SchemaRegistry() callers in helen_os/ have been migrated to explicit paths AND their test fixtures have been updated to validate against the constitutional schema shape (C6, C7) — no more ghost passes against the wrong authority. Seam remains BLOCKED on C8: the legacy root-level schemas/ directory still holds 55 files. Next cycle: Actions 3 and 5–9. When C8 flips to PASS, a V6 CLOSED receipt becomes issuable.
